title: Tectaria aenigma
taxonomy: Tectaria aenigma is classified within the family Dryopteridaceae. This family is part of the order Polypodiales, which is a group of ferns. The order Polypodiales is further classified under the class Polypodiopsida, which comprises over 1,000 species of ferns. The class Polypodiopsida is part of the division Pteridophyta, also known as the fern division. This division is a group of vascular plants that reproduce via spores.
uses: Tectaria aenigma, a species of fern, has been reported to have various medicinal uses in some regions. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues due to its antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of the plant are often used to make tea, which is believed to have soothing effects on the body. Furthermore, Tectaria aenigma has been used in folk medicine to treat skin conditions and wounds, thanks to its antimicrobial properties. However, more research is needed to fully understand the efficacy and potential side effects of using this plant for medicinal purposes.
